title: clippy collapsible_match warning in config.rs env-interpolation
description:
`cargo clippy --lib --tests` on main emits one `collapsible_match` warning at
`src/config.rs:632` (the OS-env interpolation walk from bd-7b1dd4): an inner
`if s.contains('$')` inside a `match serde_yaml::Value::String(s)` arm that
clippy suggests collapsing into a match guard `String(s) if s.contains('$')`.
Pre-existing on main (not a regression); harmless, but it will make a future
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` CI gate (bd-baba99) red until fixed. One-line
change. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-700306 session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

title: Context: store load/merge
description: Parent epic bd-7a1d2f (label `context`). Precedence: `--context-file` > `--session-file` merge > `NLIR_CONTEXT` env > default file.

title: Parallelism: context-write serialization

title: Docs: error-message & diagnostics polish

title: Decide where MessageViews SPEC defaults live (config resolve vs messages fallback)
description: SPEC §Example config defines canonical `context.messages.views` defaults (`^`=[assistant], `^_`=[user], `^*`=[user,assistant,system], `^/`=[system]), but config `MessageViews::default()` is all-empty and `resolve_defaults` (src/config.rs) does not populate them. So the built-in `^` behaviour (and the SPEC `msg` test `^-1`) only works because src/messages.rs `effective_roles()` bakes those SPEC defaults as a fallback for an empty configured view. That's a reasonable seam, but it splits "view defaults" across two modules. Decision for the config owner: either (a) centralize the SPEC view defaults in config defaults-resolution (then messages could drop its fallback), or (b) keep them in the messages layer and document config `MessageViews::default()` as intentionally empty. Either is fine; flagging so the two don't silently diverge. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-f9809a/bd-e8064e/bd-43ac5e session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: Eval: DAG evaluator (operand-first)
description: Parent epic bd-2b226d (label `eval`). Bottom-up; children resolve to values before the parent op runs.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Lexer: operator longest-match tokenising
description: Parent epic bd-c46071 (label `lexer`). Longest-match configured ops so `**` beats `*`; ops are non-alphanumeric.

title: Eval: template realisation
description: Parent epic bd-2b226d (label `eval`). `%` (arity-1 raw), `%0`/`%1` (fixed arity>1), `%%` literal.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Shared temp-file test helper to dedup temp_dir()+pid+nanos idiom
description: Context/config unit tests each re-implement the same throwaway-temp-file idiom: `std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("nlir-<tag>-{}-{nanos}", std::process::id()))` + manual `fs::remove_file` cleanup (see src/config.rs tests and the new src/context.rs tests, ~6 call-sites). A tiny shared test helper (e.g. a `TempFile` RAII guard, or a `test_util` module behind `#[cfg(test)]`) would dedup the boilerplate, guarantee cleanup on panic, and avoid pid/nanos-collision copy-paste drift. No `tempfile` dev-dep is present; a ~20-line in-tree helper avoids adding one. Low priority long-tail polish. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-0909ab/bd-fdd3bc session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

Child scope (label `eval`): DAG evaluator (operand-first); realisation resolution (`command`/`reduce`/`template`/`join`/`model`) + mode selection; template realisation (`%`, `%0`/`%1`, `%%`); join realisation (variadic); numeric `reduce` builtins (add/sub/mul/div/pow); command realisation (`NLIR_ARGS`, stdout); operand type coercion before apply; list rendering via `_sep` + spread into variadic ops; nullary-pop stack consumption.

title: CI: self-hosted release workflow on v* tag push
description: Parent epic bd-e0a557 (label `release`). `.github/workflows/release.yml` on self-hosted runner(s), triggered by `v*` tags: build `nlir` binaries via the flake (linux x86_64/aarch64, darwin arm64 as applicable) plus `.sha256` sidecars, stream assets straight into a DRAFT GitHub release, then publish after an asset-inspection gate confirms the expected binary set (the stream-to-draft-then-inspect pattern). Mirror `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`kittui`/`ring-mods` `release.yml`; see the cacophony SPEC release section for the streaming/inspection rationale. Keep runners self-hosted with the correct labels.

title: Dedup negative-index-from-end resolver (stack + messages resolve_index)
description: `Stack::resolve_index` (src/stack.rs) and `messages::resolve_index` (src/messages.rs) are the identical negative-index-from-end resolver: `index >= 0` from the start, `index < 0` from the end (`-1` = last), `None` out of range. Both back SPEC index semantics (`$N` stack index with `$0` bottom / `$-1` top; `^N` message index). A shared helper (e.g. `fn resolve_index(len, i64) -> Option<usize>` in a small `index`/util module, or one made pub and reused) would dedup ~6 lines and keep the semantics in one place. Low priority; the two are currently in-sync. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-d4631b/bd-22fa7e session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1

title: Parser+lexer: key=RHS assignment (Expr::Assign) + _-prefixed keys
description: SPEC §Context: read & assign. Parser: add Expr::Assign{key,value}; `key=RHS` where key is a literal identifier (Bare, incl `_`-prefixed system key), `=` is the builtin Equals token (lowest precedence, right-assoc), RHS is an expression. Yields the value; writes immediately (eval-side is aur-1's bd-c85dee). Lexer: allow `_`-prefixed bare keys so `_sep=`/`_cache=` lex. Unblocks aur-1 evaluator `=` handling + context bd-c85dee. Parser epic bd-ab25f7 was closed; this is a small follow-on in the parser/lexer lane.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: Lexer: role-modifier & negative-index disambiguation

title: Project skeleton: Nix flake + Rust `nlir` CLI with crate deps wired
description:
Foundational bead. `SPEC.md` at the repo root is the normative contract for the whole project — read it first; all beads implement it.

Set up the initial project scaffolding so every downstream bead has somewhere to land:

- **Nix flake** managing a Rust CLI, following the same pattern as the other harryaskham Rust CLIs. Use the **`omni-cli`** project as the primary reference for the flake + CLI structure (mature examples also live under `~/.cacophony/daemon/checkouts/…`, e.g. `tendril`, `kittui`).
- **Rust binary crate `nlir`** with the command-tree scaffolding and stub subcommands from SPEC §CLI surface: `-e`, `parse`, `test`, `repl`, `set`, `get`, `append-message`, `mcp stdio`, `self-update`, `feedback`, plus global flags (`--config`, `--context-file`, `--session-file`, `--mode`, `--model`, `--parallelism`, `--quiet`, `--dry-run`).
- **Wire crate dependencies on the friend-project template stack** (these are configured Cacophony friend projects / sub-crates):
  - `mcp-cli` — gives the MCP server almost for free (`nlir mcp stdio`).
  - `updatable-cli` — `nlir self-update`.
  - `feedback-cli` — `nlir feedback`.
  Reference how `omni-cli` consumes these three crates and mirror that wiring.

Acceptance: `nix build`, `nix flake check`, and `cargo build` are green; `nlir --help` lists the command tree; the three template subcommands are wired (even if their handlers are thin) so later beads only fill in behaviour.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: fresh agent checkouts lack a git author identity for direct commits
description:
A managed agent checkout has no git `user.name`/`user.email` configured, so the
first direct `git commit` a dev worker makes fails with "Author identity
unknown ... Please tell me who you are" until the agent sets a local identity.
Prior mainline commits are authored `Cacophony Daemon <daemon@cacophony.local>`,
so workers must discover and replicate that convention by hand. Suggested fix:
seed a repo-local git identity (matching the daemon convention) at checkout
creation, or document the expected identity in AGENTS/SPEC. May be a
cacophony-harness concern rather than nlir-specific. Filed via reflect-session
mixin from bd-700306 session.
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-2

Mirror the `omni-cli` usage of each crate.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [build] Linux x86 nix dev-shell coreutils segfaulting
description:
Reported by Harry (2026-07-03): on Linux x86, coreutils tools segfault inside the nlir `nix develop` dev shell, blocking `nix develop --command cargo build` for the Aurora agents.

Context / likely cause:
- nlir's flake pins nixpkgs to collective's exact rev (flakehub tarball rev 0590cd39) per operator request, to reuse the cached toolchain.
- The dev shell is only required on macOS to supply `libiconv` for the `-liconv` link (ureq/rustls TLS stack via feedback-cli/updatable-cli). On Linux, glibc already provides iconv.

WORKAROUND for Linux x86 (unblocks Aurora agents now):
- Build WITHOUT the dev shell: plain `cargo build` with a system Rust toolchain should link fine on Linux (no `-liconv` needed). Then `cargo fmt`/`clippy`/`test` directly.
- If a system Rust toolchain is unavailable, the nix rustc/cargo binaries themselves are fine; only coreutils in the interactive shell env is segfaulting — invoke tools by absolute path or use `nix build .#nlir` for a one-shot binary.

Diagnostics needed (from a Linux x86 node):
1. Is this nlir-specific or do OTHER cacophony project dev shells (omni-cli, tendril, collective) also segfault coreutils on the same node? If all projects, it is environmental (node/store), not the nlir flake.
2. Which exact coreutils tool + command segfaults (e.g. `cp`, `mkdir`, `cat`)? `dmesg`/exit signal?
3. Does `nix store verify --repair /nix/store/...coreutils...` or re-fetch fix it (partial/corrupt download)?
4. Does omni-cli (which pins github nixos-unstable, a NEWER nixpkgs) work on the same node? If yes, the pinned rev 0590cd39's coreutils is the culprit on that hardware and we may need a devShell nixpkgs bump for Linux.

Filed from the nlir config-vertical session (msm-0). Not claimed: needs a Linux x86 repro to fix.

MITIGATION LANDED (Harry, c08b7a9): removed `use flake` from `.envrc` so direnv no longer auto-loads the nix dev shell on `cd`. Practical build workflow now:
- Linux x86: build with the SYSTEM Rust toolchain — plain `cargo build` / `cargo test` / `cargo clippy` / `cargo fmt`. No dev shell needed (glibc provides iconv), which sidesteps the segfaulting dev-shell coreutils.
- macOS: still needs `libiconv`, so build via an explicit `nix develop --command cargo build` (the dev shell is fine on macOS; only Linux x86 coreutils segfaults).
Bead stays OPEN as a tracked known-issue: the underlying reason the pinned-rev dev-shell coreutils segfaults on Linux x86 is still undiagnosed and could resurface for `nix build`/CI. Diagnostics in the original description still wanted.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:ms-mac-nlir-nlir-dev-msm-0

title: [EPIC] Parser & DAG construction
description: SPEC §Mental model, §Grammar & parsing. Precedence-climbing (Pratt) parser driven by config priority/fixity, producing a DAG of statements. Child scope (label `parser`): Pratt core; prefix/postfix/infix + arity; variadic flattening; mixfix unification (infix / prefix-on-list / postfix-on-list / nullary-pop); grouping `(…)` + list literals `[a,b,c]`; statement split `;` + DAG build; backtick serial-marker (low-precedence prefix over whole RHS); `nlir parse` AST/DAG dump.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Config loading, schema & validation
description: SPEC §Modes, §Types & coercion, §Example config.yaml. The `~/.config/nlir/config.yaml` layer that defines the whole language (models, prompts, operators, context, sessions, types, defaults, tests). Child scope (label `config`): serde schema structs; config discovery + load with `--config` override and default path; OS-env interpolation at load (`$FOO`/`${FOO}`); validation (required fields, unknown keys, operator op/arity/fixity sanity, reserved-sigil collisions); defaults resolution (mode/model/parallelism, context `_sep`/`_cache`).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] LLM model backends & prompt pipeline
description: SPEC §Prompt templating pipeline, §Example config (models/prompts). The `llm`-mode realisation. Child scope (label `llm`): model resolution (alias→model, default model); `anthropic_messages` backend (direct HTTP, `json_schema` structured output, `result_field`); `command` backend (subprocess, `format` json/text, `result_field`); prompt fragments (`prompts:` env-named); `%` operand substitution (arity-1 `<text>`, variadic `<text n=k>`, `%%`); `${NLIR_*}` assembly (`NLIR_PROMPT` + system/structured/unstructured + `NLIR_ARGS`); result extraction (json `result_field` vs raw stdout text).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: LLM: prompt fragments
description: Parent epic bd-b71b0b (label `llm`). `prompts:` named fragments as env vars (system/structured/unstructured).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: [EPIC] Test runner
description: SPEC §Example config (`tests:`). Child scope (label `testrunner`): `nlir test` executes the config `tests:` block (each entry: `mode`, `expr`, `expected`, optional inline `context`/`_messages`); run in a temporary context, compare output, report pass/fail with diffs; suitable as the offline `det`-mode regression gate.

title: [EPIC] Lexer / tokeniser
description: SPEC §Whitespace/escapes/quoting, §Grammar (Tokens). Turn shorthand text into a token stream. Child scope (label `lexer`): non-semantic whitespace stripping between tokens; bare `[a-zA-Z0-9]+` and numeric literals; quoted literals `'…'` (raw) / `"…"` (escapes processed) with POSIX escapes (`\n \t \  \\ \" \'`); operator longest-match (`**` before `*`); builtin sigils `; $ ^ = [ ] , ( )` + backtick and sub-forms (`$name`/`$N`, `^`/`^_`/`^*`/`^/`, `key=`); role-modifier and negative-index disambiguation after `^`/`$`.

title: Lexer: `_` echo operator can't tokenise (lex_bare consumes `_` mid-token)
description:
The SPEC `_` echo operator (op: "_", the det-echo test `xxx_2` → "xxx xxx") cannot be tokenised. lex_bare (src/lexer.rs ~line 369) treats `_` as a bare-token continuation char (`c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_'`) — a consequence of allowing `_`-prefixed system keys like `_sep`. So `xxx_2` lexes as a single Bare("xxx_2") and the configured `_` operator never splits it; the evaluator returns the input unchanged.

Repro: with a config operator `echo: { op: "_", arity: 2, fixity: infix, command: ... }`, `nlir -e 'xxx_2'` (det) yields "xxx_2" instead of "xxx xxx".

This blocks SPEC det-echo. The fix is in the lexer lane (msm-0): disambiguate the `_` echo operator from `_`-in-bare-token / `_`-prefixed keys — e.g. only treat leading `_` as a bare/key start (not a mid-token continuation), or let a configured operator sigil `_` win when it appears between operands, while `_name` at a key position still lexes as a key. Command realisation itself (bd-3c1e6d) is verified working via a non-`_` sigil in src/eval.rs tests; only the `_`-sigil lexing is blocked. Filed by aur-1 from the eval SPEC-completion batch (bd-c85dee/bd-02a795/bd-3c1e6d).
creator: aurora:nlir:aurora-nlir-nlir-dev-aur-1
spoken_name: lexer underscore echo operator tokenization bug

title: Release: wire updatable-cli end-to-end so `nlir self-update` / `nlir update` installs the latest tagged release
description: Parent epic bd-e0a557 (label `release`). Completes the plumbing stub bd-1b0283 and makes the whole update path actually work: point updatable-cli at `harryaskham/nlir` with asset names matching the release workflow, then version-check + download + sha256-verify + atomic stage-`nlir_next`-then-promote install, and verify `nlir self-update` (and the `update` alias if exposed) upgrades onto a real published tag. Reference `omni-cli`/`tendril`/`ring-mods` self-update wiring (the `*_self_update_*` surfaces). Depends on the release-workflow bead (needs published assets to update onto).
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: CLI: context-source precedence
description:
Parent epic bd-bc848a (label `cli`). `--context-file` > `--session-file` > env > default file.

[note from aur-1, bd-0909ab context store landing] The precedence LOGIC now lives in `nlir::context::Context::load(LoadSources, &ContextConfig)` (src/context.rs). This CLI bead is the wiring layer: resolve the ambient env into a `LoadSources` and call `Context::load`. Semantics the store already implements (strict first-present-wins): `--context-file` > session import > `NLIR_CONTEXT` env > default file. Write-through targets: `--context-file` and the default file are file-backed; `NLIR_CONTEXT` env and session import are TRANSIENT (in-memory only, no write-through). Session-file *format parsing* is NOT this bead nor the store — it belongs to the sessions epic (bd-720cdb parse → Map, bd-000666 merge); that Map is handed to `LoadSources.session`. `default_context_path(&cfg, HOME)` + `expand_tilde` resolve `context.file_default` (tilde-expanded). SPEC clarity gap surfaced while implementing: the SPEC §CLI list writes "--session-file merge" which could read as merge-on-top-of-lower-precedence rather than strict-precedence; the store chose strict first-present-wins — confirm that's the intended reading when wiring this.
creator: ms-mac:nlir:harryaskham

title: Messages: role-filtered views
description: Parent epic bd-93dd1a (label `messages`). `^`=assistant, `^_`=user, `^*`=all, `^/`=system from config views.
